title: Migrate Legacy SPL Vault Receipts To V2
summary: Repair a vault upgrade path that must migrate legacy vault configs and per-user receipt accounts to a stricter V2 layout without losing balances or weakening authorization.
businessLogic: A vault holds one SPL mint under a PDA authority and tracks per-user balances through receipt PDAs.
Legacy V1 vaults and receipts already exist in production and must be migrated in place to V2 instead of rebuilt from scratch.
V2 adds stricter pause semantics, explicit version checks, and receipt withdrawal-history accounting that must survive migration intact.

instructions.name: initialize_legacy_vault
instructions.description: Create a legacy V1 vault config and PDA-controlled custody token account so tests can simulate pre-upgrade state.

instructions.name: initialize_vault
instructions.description: Create a fresh V2 vault directly for new deployments.

instructions.name: migrate_vault
instructions.description: Upgrade a V1 vault config to V2 without changing its seed, admin, mint, PDA authority, or accounting totals.

instructions.name: deposit
instructions.description: Deposit user funds into the vault and create or update a receipt PDA using the correct version for the vault state.

instructions.name: migrate_receipt
instructions.description: Upgrade a legacy user receipt to V2 in place while preserving owner binding, balance, and withdrawal history.

instructions.name: withdraw
instructions.description: Allow withdrawals only from migrated V2 receipts into a token account actually owned by the withdrawing user.

instructions.name: set_paused
instructions.description: Allow only the vault admin to pause or resume V2 vault movement.

invariants: Only the configured admin may migrate a legacy vault or pause a V2 vault.
Only the stored receipt owner may migrate or withdraw from that receipt.
Migrating a vault or receipt must preserve existing balances and withdrawal totals instead of resetting them.
A V2 vault must reject withdrawals from receipts that were never migrated to V2.
Withdrawals must go only to a token account owned by the withdrawing user and using the configured mint.
failureConditions: Resetting `total_withdrawn` or per-receipt withdrawal history during repeat migration calls.
Allowing an attacker to claim ownership of another user's legacy receipt during migration.
Allowing a non-admin signer to migrate the vault.
Allowing withdrawals from a legacy receipt after the vault has already been upgraded to V2.
Allowing migrated funds to be redirected into an attacker-owned token account.
